chunk_summary: This section focuses on macronutrient requirements for toddlers aged 1-3 years, emphasizing age-specific dietary guidelines and the prevention of non-communicable diseases through balanced nutrition and reduced junk food consumption.
learning_objectives: Understand the macronutrient needs of toddlers aged 1-3 years for optimal growth and development.
Learn age-specific dietary recommendations from authoritative guidelines such as ICMR-NIN and WHO.
Identify strategies to prevent obesity and non-communicable diseases in early childhood through nutrition.
Explore the role of complementary feeding in toddlers' diets.
key_facts: Toddlers aged 1-3 years require approximately 1,000-1,400 kcal/day depending on activity level (ICMR-NIN, 2024).
Protein intake should be 1.2 g/kg/day to support growth and development (ICMR-NIN, 2024).
Fat should contribute 30-40% of total energy intake, with emphasis on unsaturated fats (WHO, 2023).
Carbohydrates should make up 50-60% of total energy intake, prioritizing complex carbohydrates (ICMR-NIN, 2024).
Limiting junk food consumption is critical to prevent obesity and related diseases (Indian Pediatrics, 2019).
reference_quote: Limiting junk food consumption is critical to prevent obesity and related diseases in children (Indian Pediatrics, 2019).
document_key_topics: Macronutrient requirements for toddlers aged 1-3 years
Prevention of obesity and non-communicable diseases
